+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION MARKS KEY MILESTONE WITH ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER

FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

urn:contentItem:6JGF-M5B3-RS42-W4H2-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

+ VPM, CLARK CONSTRUCTION UNVEIL NEW HEADQUARTERS AIMED AT FUTURE BUILDERS

VPM welcomed students from the Metropolitan Business League to its new headquarters, which Clark Construction is building. The event, part of a 'Youth in Construction' initiative, took place on 19th November 2025 and gave Richmond Technical Center students an opportunity to explore trades across the construction sector. During the tour, attendees were introduced to carpentry, electrical work, HVAC, and plumbing. The program also highlighted Baker Concrete's apprenticeship program, illustrating the variety of career paths available. This practical experience enabled students to picture themselves working on future projects of a similar scale. VPM broke ground on the new headquarters in August 2024, with an expected completion in 2026. The Monroe Ward relocation is meant to centralize VPM’s news and content production, increasing public access and engagement. By deepening its footprint in Richmond, VPM seeks to enhance local coverage of news and events. Beyond trades, the students interacted with VPM's broadcasting capabilities, recording clips in the mobile sound booth to gain a firsthand sense of the organization's daily operations that deliver news to millions via TV and radio channels. Community support is essential for VPM, helping sustain local news, educational programming, and inspiring content. As VPM advances its work with Clark Construction, the partnership demonstrates how such initiatives can enrich local education and industry awareness.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ TRENT JOHNSON NAMED VICE PRESIDENT OF AVIATION OPERATIONS FOR BALFOUR BEATTY IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026. In the newly expanded role, he will lead and grow the company's aviation practice across North and South Carolina, leveraging nearly two decades in the construction industry. Johnson brings 12-and-a-half years with Balfour Beatty and a total of 19 years in the field. He has delivered 22 aviation projects, including targeted renovations and multi-phase programmes, with notable work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport, helping to solidify the company's aviation footprint in the region. In the new role, he will oversee the aviation segment across the Carolinas, continuing to support existing clients while pursuing opportunities at additional regional and international airports. His focus will include guiding project teams through active airport environments, prioritising safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination. Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, lauded Johnson for building a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner, and said his leadership will drive the next phase of growth for the company's aviation portfolio. Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and has been active in the community, notably with the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK SECURES ROLE ON BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT NETWORK RAIL

Mainmark UK has been awarded a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the geotechnical contractor to support Network Rail’s CP7 investment programme running from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an overall envelope of around £43-45 billion across Great Britain.CP7 is designed to maintain, renew, and modernise the railway, delivering a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable network. Investment priorities include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives to improve both freight and passenger performance.Within the CP7 Framework, contractors will undertake a mix of activities such as low complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will take on geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, commented, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans."Mainmark is renowned for its Teretek resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground and fills subsurface voids with minimal disruption. As heavier freight loads and severe weather stress railway assets, Teretek helps preserve structural integrity and asset resilience. The CP7 appointment positions Mainmark to contribute to a more resilient UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY POSTS 9% EPS RISE AS UK-US PROJECTS SUPPORT REVENUE GROWTH

Balfour Beatty PLC posted strong growth, powered by its construction arm, notably power-transmission work. The group’s market value has climbed about 66% over the past year as it rides a record order book, while underlying earnings per share rose 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the group said top-line growth was supported by UK demand for power transmission and a surge in US building activity. Operating profits in the support services segment jumped a third to £122m, and the underlying margin rose 80 basis points to 8.5%. The UK construction business also exceeded its margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, rising building volumes helped the group grow, though it faced cost overruns on a Civils project. The company maintains a dual-market strategy designed to sustain profitability amid these challenges. Analysts remain upbeat about the outlook, noting the large and expanding order book amid ongoing infrastructure investment. Balfour Beatty aims to improve margins and deliver projects on time, reinforcing its position in both the UK and US markets. Looking ahead, the company sees opportunity in expanding power grids and broader construction demand, while remaining mindful of rising costs and project-management challenges that could affect near-term performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION GUIDES SOUTH FLORIDA'S GROWTH THROUGH COLLABORATION AND TECH

On the 28th of March 2026, during an industry roundtable hosted in partnership with the South Florida Business Journal, leaders from development, construction, and government agencies explored how South Florida’s skyline is evolving and the role DPR Construction is playing in guiding that change.Collaboration Reshaping Project Delivery: Participants stressed that cross sector partnerships have shifted from optional to essential for navigating modern urban development. By aligning stakeholders early, projects gain measurable improvements in efficiency, safety, and quality. In a market with limited land, rising costs, and increasing regulatory demands, integrated approaches are critical to delivering long term community value rather than just buildings.Innovation and Technology Driving Efficiency: DPR Construction and its partners are leveraging prefabrication, modular construction, and emerging technologies like artificial intelligence to streamline workflows, reduce waste, and shorten lead times. Digital tools and data driven decision making support better forecasting and coordination across complex, multi phase programs, while improving site safety and accuracy.Addressing Workforce and Growth Challenges: South Florida’s strong population and economy are translating into a growing need for skilled labor. Industry leaders highlighted partnerships with schools and training programs designed to attract, develop, and retain talent within the construction sector, ensuring a steady pipeline as investment accelerates.Building for Long-Term Resilience: Finally, the discussion centered on sustainable building practices, energy efficiency, and climate resilient infrastructure. Developers and contractors aim to design communities that endure large scale change, balancing speed, cost, and sustainability. DPR Construction is positioned at the forefront of this evolution, setting benchmarks for large scale urban development in high demand markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

value.Title: First energy fund project powers up

urn:contentItem:6JJM-GMJ3-S5NM-W0GX-00000-00First energy fund project powers up2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:20ZLexisNexisAPg. A008First energy fund project powers upClaire HaoStaff Writer

The Texas Energy Fund, a controversial $10 billion taxpayer-backed program, has officially produced its first natural gas power plant for the state's power grid after some initial hiccups. The power plant, dubbed Pin Oak Creek, was announced in 2023 by Houston-based Calpine, a company that has since been acquired by Baltimore-based Constellation. The facility is located in Freestone County in central Texas and adds 460 megawatts of electricity generation, enough to power approximately 115,000 homes during the hottest summer hours.

The Texas Energy Fund, a controversial $10 billion taxpayer-backed program, has officially produced its first natural gas power plant for the state's power grid after some initial hiccups.

The power plant, dubbed Pin Oak Creek, was announced in 2023 by Houston-based Calpine, a company that has since been acquired by Baltimore-based Constellation. The facility is located in Freestone County in central Texas and adds 460 megawatts of electricity generation, enough to power approximately 115,000 homes during the hottest summer hours.

NRG Energy, another Houston-based power generation company, expects to bring online one of its Texas Energy Fund-backed power plants located in Houston later this summer.

At a Tuesday news conference, Gov. Greg Abbott said the Pin Oak Creek power plant coming online was "a tremendous day for the state of Texas."

"There's no reason why the Texas power grid will ever run out of power again," Abbott said, referencing the 2021 winter storm that knocked out all kinds of power plants and triggered mass outages across the state.

Most of the Texas Energy Fund is meant to support up to 10,000 megawatts of natural gas power plants through grants and low-interest loans. Pin Oak Creek is the first project receiving such a loan to start operations, nearly three years after voters approved the program.

In that time, the fund has faced a number of project dropouts, messy fraud allegations and withholding of public records, as well as criticism that it's not needed or too slow in bringing projects online.

"It might come with a name that sounds nice and beneficial," advocacy group Public Citizen wrote last year of the Texas Energy Fund. "Still, the details tell a different story, one of a state government intent on using tax dollars to prop up the fossil fuel industry in its losing battle against renewable energy."

Through it all, Texas politicians have championed the program as critical in achieving one of their key energy priorities: encouraging the build-out of more natural gas power plants.

In recent years, most of the new power supply that's been added to the state's electric grid has been solar arrays and battery storage, which push down electricity prices and make it less economic to build fossil fuel power plants.

At various points, Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick has called the Texas Energy Fund his "baby" and threatened that the state would build its own power plants if the private sector didn't step up.

In his remarks on Tuesday in Fairfield, Abbott cheered Texas as "America's undisputed power leader" -- number one in oil and gas production, wind power generation, utility-scale solar power and utility-scale battery storage.

Natural gas power plants like Pin Oak Creek, however, are still needed to ensure there's plentiful electricity regardless of sun or wind availability, Abbott said.

"With projects like this, it doesn't matter what time of the day it is, Texas is going to have plenty of power to make sure that power remains on for every household, every business across the state of Texas," Abbott said.

Calpine CEO Andrew Novotny said the company originally bought the turbines in the Pin Oak Creek plant more than 10 years ago, "but the market at the time didn't need us back then." So, it waited until market conditions changed a few years ago, as electricity demand in Texas began surging from economic and population growth.

"Pin Oak Creek is designed to operate when demand is at its highest and reliability matters the most. It's not meant to run all the time, and it's also not built to serve just a single (electricity user)," Novotny said.

David Dardis, chief external affairs and growth officer for Constellation, said increasing the company's capabilities in the fast-growing Texas market was why it decided to acquire Calpine, previously Houston's largest private company.

"When Constellation first envisioned combining with Calpine, it was projects like the Pin Oak Creek facility here in Texas that was a key part of the strategic rationale for the transaction," he said.

value.Title: May 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-S813-S8K8-R2R8-00000-00May 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS

On February 25, 2026, Consigli Construction Co. commenced work on the Williams College Museum of Art, marking the institution's first purpose-built facility on campus. Abigail Berkebile, a figure at Consigli Construction, highlighted the complexity and unique design of the project, which aims to enhance student access to art collections. Berkebile also utilized the announcement to emphasize the diverse opportunities available for women in the construction industry, noting that project teams rely on broad expertise spanning accounting, legal matters, IT, and procurement, allowing professionals to advance without a traditional field background.

In peer news, Suffolk Construction continues to manage the Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project, which facilitated the reopening of a long-term westbound lane closure on U.S. Route 58 in Virginia on May 5, 2026. The project is designed to build a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to improve safety and regional efficiency. Separately, on May 1, 2026, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement to source renewable energy from a 70-megawatt solar farm currently under construction in Suffolk. The solar farm is anticipated to commence power generation in 2027 and will supply approximately 5 percent of the telecommunications company's total energy demands as it targets Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040.

Turner Construction Company achieved a significant milestone on April 28, 2026, with the topping out of the 450,000-square-foot, 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company, stated, "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame. It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner's commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." On the same day, Turner Construction announced the start of vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Pennsylvania. The project features an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will increase capacity to 500 beds by the first quarter of 2028. Additionally, on April 22, 2026, Turner was named the general contractor for the $500 million Westcourt Orlando mixed-use development, which will feature a hotel, residential units, and an entertainment venue.

Gilbane Development announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets on April 28, 2026. Operating out of the New York City office, Vlahakis is tasked with refining the firm's funding strategy and leading transaction structuring. In other company developments, Gilbane launched leasing for Phase II of its $135 million affordable housing redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Washington, D.C., on April 3, 2026. Slated for completion by summer 2026, the second phase comprises 229 units across four buildings, with twenty percent of the apartments designated as Permanent Supportive Housing managed by the district's Department of Homeless Services.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-SDG3-RRVD-714K-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FOUNDATION SIGNALS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN RESKILLING AS AI ADVANCES

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

+ CROSS-SECTOR COLLABORATION AND FUNDING DRIVE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S BALTIMORE ECONOMIC AGENDA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business to Baltimore to discuss strategies for strengthening the economy and expanding opportunity.A centerpiece announcement was a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, with a target of about 1.6 million new jobs across sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence.The Foundation also committed $12 million to Invest in Our Future to promote clean energy opportunities and growth in underserved areas, while Maryland Governor Wes Moore announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards for education and childcare in high-poverty areas. The event also highlighted the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and emphasized partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next gathering is planned for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ WINTER PROPOSES GLOBAL DATA COLLABORATION TO STRENGTHEN ONE HEALTH FOR CHILDREN

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for data unification at the One Health Summit. He told attendees that despite vast data resources, information remains siloed across sectors, hindering decisions and allocation of resources, and he reiterated the need for cross sector collaboration with a quote: "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter said the call for data integration aligns with earlier remarks by Oracle co founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations amid about 3,000 databases. For illustration, he cited the drought in Somalia: the Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator developing a dashboard that links data from health, nutrition, climate and agriculture to inform local actors and international donors, including the diaspora contributing roughly $2 billion annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC APPLAUDS FORD FOUNDATION'S LONGSTANDING PARTNERSHIP IN NIGERIAN HUMAN RIGHTS WORK

On Apr. 28, 2026, the NHRC in Abuja recognized the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its long-running support to human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony brought together Commission executives and was attended by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu lauded the Foundation for backing efforts to expand access to justice and to strengthen the Commission's governance and operations. He noted that tackling Nigeria's human rights challenges requires sustained cooperation with development partners to translate policy into practical outcomes for citizens.Aniagolu-Okoye said the recognition underscores the Foundation's ongoing commitment to both awareness and practical justice. She emphasized that rights education must be paired with strong enforcement mechanisms, and she highlighted the Foundation's role in supporting citizens to seek remedy when rights are violated.The event underscored the value of continuing public-private collaboration, with Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, acknowledging the MOVE project and the dashboard system as key areas advanced with Foundation support.

+ SOFT POWER OF ECOWAS FIRST LADIES HERALDED AS KEY TO GBV REFORMS, FORD FOUNDATION WARNS

The Ford Foundation urged First Ladies at the ECOWAS Forum on 24th April 2026 to harness their influence to combat gender-based violence, arguing that the soft power of First Ladies can drive social change and strengthen accountability.It highlighted the continued prevalence of GBV, noting that one in three women globally are affected and that child marriage remains a concern in West and Central Africa, with many girls married before 18.Economic costs were cited as a compelling reason for action, with GBV-related losses reaching up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, surpassing investments in some priority sectors such as education.While First Ladies are not formal policymakers, the Ford Foundation stressed their essential role in shaping public discourse and decision-making, pointing to Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as leaders whose campaigns elevated women’s issues and prompted national responses. Such examples demonstrate how strategic use of soft power can catalyze systemic change.The Foundation reiterated ongoing investments in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, including partnerships with First Ladies and governors’ spouses to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based services, and challenge harmful norms. It emphasized that laws alone are insufficient without public trust and accessible support for survivors.

+ FORD FOUNDATION BETS $60 MILLION ON CIVIC PARTICIPATION AND ELECTION INTEGRITY

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to bolster democracy in the United States, announced on April 14, 2026. The grants will fund nonpartisan groups focused on expanding civic participation and protecting voting rights, across the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The program builds on the foundation's Civic Engagement and Government effort, which has awarded more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the move as a proactive step to safeguard the integrity of elections and public trust, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized voting access as essential, and Pillars of the Community co-chairs Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer highlighted bipartisan support for electoral integrity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-0NR3-RVT7-63BW-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:20ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FOUNDATION SIGNALS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN RESKILLING AS AI ADVANCES

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

+ CROSS-SECTOR COLLABORATION AND FUNDING DRIVE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S BALTIMORE ECONOMIC AGENDA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business to Baltimore to discuss strategies for strengthening the economy and expanding opportunity.A centerpiece announcement was a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, with a target of about 1.6 million new jobs across sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence.The Foundation also committed $12 million to Invest in Our Future to promote clean energy opportunities and growth in underserved areas, while Maryland Governor Wes Moore announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards for education and childcare in high-poverty areas. The event also highlighted the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and emphasized partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next gathering is planned for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ WINTER PROPOSES GLOBAL DATA COLLABORATION TO STRENGTHEN ONE HEALTH FOR CHILDREN

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for data unification at the One Health Summit. He told attendees that despite vast data resources, information remains siloed across sectors, hindering decisions and allocation of resources, and he reiterated the need for cross sector collaboration with a quote: "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter said the call for data integration aligns with earlier remarks by Oracle co founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations amid about 3,000 databases. For illustration, he cited the drought in Somalia: the Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator developing a dashboard that links data from health, nutrition, climate and agriculture to inform local actors and international donors, including the diaspora contributing roughly $2 billion annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC APPLAUDS FORD FOUNDATION'S LONGSTANDING PARTNERSHIP IN NIGERIAN HUMAN RIGHTS WORK

On Apr. 28, 2026, the NHRC in Abuja recognized the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its long-running support to human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony brought together Commission executives and was attended by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu lauded the Foundation for backing efforts to expand access to justice and to strengthen the Commission's governance and operations. He noted that tackling Nigeria's human rights challenges requires sustained cooperation with development partners to translate policy into practical outcomes for citizens.Aniagolu-Okoye said the recognition underscores the Foundation's ongoing commitment to both awareness and practical justice. She emphasized that rights education must be paired with strong enforcement mechanisms, and she highlighted the Foundation's role in supporting citizens to seek remedy when rights are violated.The event underscored the value of continuing public-private collaboration, with Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, acknowledging the MOVE project and the dashboard system as key areas advanced with Foundation support.

+ SOFT POWER OF ECOWAS FIRST LADIES HERALDED AS KEY TO GBV REFORMS, FORD FOUNDATION WARNS

The Ford Foundation urged First Ladies at the ECOWAS Forum on 24th April 2026 to harness their influence to combat gender-based violence, arguing that the soft power of First Ladies can drive social change and strengthen accountability.It highlighted the continued prevalence of GBV, noting that one in three women globally are affected and that child marriage remains a concern in West and Central Africa, with many girls married before 18.Economic costs were cited as a compelling reason for action, with GBV-related losses reaching up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, surpassing investments in some priority sectors such as education.While First Ladies are not formal policymakers, the Ford Foundation stressed their essential role in shaping public discourse and decision-making, pointing to Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as leaders whose campaigns elevated women’s issues and prompted national responses. Such examples demonstrate how strategic use of soft power can catalyze systemic change.The Foundation reiterated ongoing investments in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, including partnerships with First Ladies and governors’ spouses to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based services, and challenge harmful norms. It emphasized that laws alone are insufficient without public trust and accessible support for survivors.

+ FORD FOUNDATION BETS $60 MILLION ON CIVIC PARTICIPATION AND ELECTION INTEGRITY

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to bolster democracy in the United States, announced on April 14, 2026. The grants will fund nonpartisan groups focused on expanding civic participation and protecting voting rights, across the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The program builds on the foundation's Civic Engagement and Government effort, which has awarded more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the move as a proactive step to safeguard the integrity of elections and public trust, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized voting access as essential, and Pillars of the Community co-chairs Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer highlighted bipartisan support for electoral integrity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH0-1DY3-RV6X-J37F-00000-00Exelon's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:50:04ZLexisNexisExelon's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

Exelon's trailing twelve months dividend is $1.62. Its dividend yield at its current price is 3.52%. Its current price is at a 9.2% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 13.2% premium to its 52 week low. If Exelon was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.99%.

In comparison, Dominion Energy, Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.67. Its dividend yield at its current price is 4.14%. Its current price is at a 3.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 28.5% premium to its 52 week low. If Dominion Energy, Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 5.32%.

$1000 invested into Exelon stock 5 years ago would be worth $2,020 today. This total gain of $1,020 is generated by a capital gain of $713 and dividends of $307.

$1000 invested into Dominion Energy, Inc. stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,243 today. This total gain of $243 is generated by a capital gain of $18 and dividends of $224.

Exelon's dividend has grown 5.7% over the last 4 years.

Dominion Energy, Inc.'s dividend has grown 0% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

Exelon Corporation operates as a holding company for regulated electric and gas utilities, primarily generating revenue through electricity transmission and distribution, which accounts for approximately 85% of total revenue. The largest revenue contributors are its PHI subsidiary, focused on Mid-Atlantic operations, and ComEd, serving northern Illinois, which together represent over 58% of the company's $24.3 billion full-year revenue. The fastest-growing segments include BGE, driven by Maryland distribution rates, and PECO, supported by Pennsylvania electric and gas rates and favorable weather conditions. These segments have shown significant year-over-year growth, reflecting the company's investments in infrastructure and regulatory recoveries.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

urn:contentItem:6JGG-3XS3-S7C2-R1H3-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FOUNDATION SIGNALS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN RESKILLING AS AI ADVANCES

The Rockefeller Foundation on 28th April 2026 unveiled a grant-based nationwide effort to address AI-related disruption in the job market, aiming to prevent displacement and guide workers toward roles that complement automation. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development and places emphasis on program design, metrics and evaluation, with plans to measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to gauge the impact of automation. Funding will be provided through grants, pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The organization notes that implementation choices will determine whether the initiative yields scalable reskilling pipelines or a patchwork of pilot projects. Viewed as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative, the foundation hopes to catalyze new models of retraining and public-private collaboration, while contributing to policy discussions on workforce development such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It anticipates a rising demand for data-driven evaluations in the skills and labor analytics ecosystem as the program unfolds.

+ CROSS-SECTOR COLLABORATION AND FUNDING DRIVE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S BALTIMORE ECONOMIC AGENDA

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business to Baltimore to discuss strategies for strengthening the economy and expanding opportunity.A centerpiece announcement was a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, with a target of about 1.6 million new jobs across sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence.The Foundation also committed $12 million to Invest in Our Future to promote clean energy opportunities and growth in underserved areas, while Maryland Governor Wes Moore announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards for education and childcare in high-poverty areas. The event also highlighted the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and emphasized partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next gathering is planned for Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ WINTER PROPOSES GLOBAL DATA COLLABORATION TO STRENGTHEN ONE HEALTH FOR CHILDREN

On April 21st, 2026, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, called for data unification at the One Health Summit. He told attendees that despite vast data resources, information remains siloed across sectors, hindering decisions and allocation of resources, and he reiterated the need for cross sector collaboration with a quote: "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter said the call for data integration aligns with earlier remarks by Oracle co founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations amid about 3,000 databases. For illustration, he cited the drought in Somalia: the Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator developing a dashboard that links data from health, nutrition, climate and agriculture to inform local actors and international donors, including the diaspora contributing roughly $2 billion annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ NHRC APPLAUDS FORD FOUNDATION'S LONGSTANDING PARTNERSHIP IN NIGERIAN HUMAN RIGHTS WORK

On Apr. 28, 2026, the NHRC in Abuja recognized the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its long-running support to human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony brought together Commission executives and was attended by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu lauded the Foundation for backing efforts to expand access to justice and to strengthen the Commission's governance and operations. He noted that tackling Nigeria's human rights challenges requires sustained cooperation with development partners to translate policy into practical outcomes for citizens.Aniagolu-Okoye said the recognition underscores the Foundation's ongoing commitment to both awareness and practical justice. She emphasized that rights education must be paired with strong enforcement mechanisms, and she highlighted the Foundation's role in supporting citizens to seek remedy when rights are violated.The event underscored the value of continuing public-private collaboration, with Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, acknowledging the MOVE project and the dashboard system as key areas advanced with Foundation support.

+ SOFT POWER OF ECOWAS FIRST LADIES HERALDED AS KEY TO GBV REFORMS, FORD FOUNDATION WARNS

The Ford Foundation urged First Ladies at the ECOWAS Forum on 24th April 2026 to harness their influence to combat gender-based violence, arguing that the soft power of First Ladies can drive social change and strengthen accountability.It highlighted the continued prevalence of GBV, noting that one in three women globally are affected and that child marriage remains a concern in West and Central Africa, with many girls married before 18.Economic costs were cited as a compelling reason for action, with GBV-related losses reaching up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, surpassing investments in some priority sectors such as education.While First Ladies are not formal policymakers, the Ford Foundation stressed their essential role in shaping public discourse and decision-making, pointing to Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as leaders whose campaigns elevated women’s issues and prompted national responses. Such examples demonstrate how strategic use of soft power can catalyze systemic change.The Foundation reiterated ongoing investments in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, including partnerships with First Ladies and governors’ spouses to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based services, and challenge harmful norms. It emphasized that laws alone are insufficient without public trust and accessible support for survivors.

+ FORD FOUNDATION BETS $60 MILLION ON CIVIC PARTICIPATION AND ELECTION INTEGRITY

The Ford Foundation unveiled a $60 million initiative to bolster democracy in the United States, announced on April 14, 2026. The grants will fund nonpartisan groups focused on expanding civic participation and protecting voting rights, across the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. The program builds on the foundation's Civic Engagement and Government effort, which has awarded more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the move as a proactive step to safeguard the integrity of elections and public trust, adding, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized voting access as essential, and Pillars of the Community co-chairs Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer highlighted bipartisan support for electoral integrity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
